parsed_review.summary: CP00-806 (Risk A, 10 units, RP26.P06.M02.S21-RP26.P06.M03.S08) is a sound descriptor-only continuation of the CP788-CP805 enterprise-SaaS hardening pattern. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash) confirms the pack is honest and not overclaiming: the manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress (the correct pre-closeout state; closed CP805 ends production_ready=true and chains next_pack_id=CP00-806 / next_subphase RP26.P06.M02.S21). I recomputed coverage from the live plan (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json) included_units and it matches the binding and requirements exactly: 10 units; deliverable distribution test 2 / security_audit 2 / implementation 6; phase RP26.P06=10; micro RP26.P06.M02=2 and RP26.P06.M03=8; titles Type And Shape Definition 2 / Primary Implementation Slice 8. Registry row constants match the plan unit titles per micro-phase (PERMISSION_MATRIX_TEST_ROWS for M02; PERMISSION_MATRIX_ROWS.slice(0,8) for M03). Boundaries hold throughout: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is spread into every freezeResult; every case-set row is descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false with raw/tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payload flags false, and ROW_EXTRAS only add further false/descriptor-only flags; validators.js actively rejects runtime permission evaluation, permission-decision/audit writes, UI/API runtime, real tenant data, cross-tenant routes, key/secret material, and enterprise-trust claims; model.test.js asserts coverage/descriptor/contract validation errors===[] and keeps the M02 cross-tenant/leak-prevention test rows and the 8 M03 implementation rows runtime-closed. SSO/SCIM descriptors are descriptor-only with all runtime/exposure flags false and validated. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is read_only and non-final (claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human approval required for runtime opening; Hermes packets are descriptor-only with emits_runtime_receipt=false / writes_product_state=false (no product-runtime Hermes; Hermes/Claude patterns serve only deterministic review packet/receipt handling). The contract promotes CP00-806 to latest_pack and latest_projection (EnterpriseSaasCp806PermissionMatrixPrimaryImplementationDescriptor) with CP805 and prior packs historical and validation.valid=true; the validate script targets CP806 and falls back to the manifest plan_binding_snapshot. No secrets/PII observed in source or the generated contract. The review readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON), and there is no pre-existing CP00-806 review evidence to reject (raw-output.json/review-receipt.json are produced by this run; the docs/ldip-integration invalid-attempt file belongs to a separate workstream and is correctly excluded). changed-file-scope is clean: the 7 active implementation refs are exactly the modified pack files, while unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, LDIP, workbook/matter docs) are correctly excluded from pack scope. One non-blocking P3 transparency caveat: because the review is strictly read-only, the dynamic validation command runs (npm test, npm run validate, closeout-pack:validate, etc.) and the downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are produced by/after this review and could not be directly executed or observed here, so this is a static-inspection PASS and the production_ready flip must wait for those green runs. No P0/P1/P2 issues; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Downstream closeout evidence artifacts and dynamic validation runs not directly observable under read-only review
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json currently exists in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-806/, and raw-output.json/review-receipt.json are not yet present in artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-806/. This is the expected state at pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending (valid_review_receipts=[]): these files are produced by/after this Claude review during closeout finalization. Because the review is strictly read-only (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash), the actual results of the validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) and the adjudication/inspection documents could not be executed or directly observed. This PASS is therefore a static-inspection PASS: the implementation (registry/service/validators/contract/test/validate-script/README/index re-export), coverage against the live plan, no-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries, and Claude/Hermes authority boundaries are internally consistent and correct as written.
findings.recommendation: During closeout finalization, generate the four required pack artifacts and confirm all listed validation commands exit 0 with valid evidence before flipping production_ready to true and committing. No code change is required for this pack.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-closeout state, not a defect in the pack. Non-blocking; will be satisfied by the deterministic closeout pipeline that consumes this review receipt. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
